vision: A comprehensive mobile companion app that empowers solo travelers to plan trips safely, discover destinations, connect with fellow travelers, and document their adventures with confidence.

target_audience.primary: Solo travelers who plan and manage their own trips, typically aged 25-45, tech-savvy, safety-conscious, and seeking adventure while maintaining independence
secondary: Backpackers and budget travelers seeking community connections
Digital nomads who need to manage long-term travel itineraries
First-time solo travelers who need extra safety features and guidance

phases.name: Foundation / MVP
phases.description: Deliver reliable core functionality with offline-first architecture, robust authentication, trip planning, safety features, and data synchronization. This phase addresses critical competitor pain points around server downtime, sync errors, and offline functionality.

milestones.title: Users can log in reliably and access the app offline
milestones.description: Robust authentication system with token refresh and full offline functionality, addressing TripIt login issues and server downtime problems

milestones.title: Users can plan multi-day trips with itineraries
milestones.description: Complete trip planning workflow with optimized performance for large itineraries, addressing Wanderlog performance issues

milestones.title: Users can stay safe with check-ins and data syncs reliably
milestones.description: Safety check-in system with reliable data sync and conflict resolution, addressing sync errors from TripIt and Wanderlog

phases.name: Core Experience Enhancement
phases.description: Enrich the user experience with destination discovery, travel journaling, navigation, and enhanced privacy controls. This phase fills the gap left by Google Trips and addresses solo-traveler needs for discovery and documentation.

milestones.title: Users can discover solo-suitable destinations
milestones.description: AI-powered destination discovery specifically for solo travelers with safety ratings and suitability filters

milestones.title: Users can document travels with photos and notes
milestones.description: Rich travel journal with photo/video uploads and centralized document storage, addressing journal app clunkiness

milestones.title: Users can navigate and control privacy
milestones.description: Google Maps integration with proper navigation and privacy-first safety features with granular controls

phases.name: Advanced Features & Growth
phases.description: Add community features, AI-powered recommendations, place verification, and monetization infrastructure. This phase addresses solo traveler community pain points and prepares the platform for growth.

milestones.title: Users can connect with other solo travelers
milestones.description: Traveler community features with verification, chat, and meetups, addressing solo travel app safety concerns

milestones.title: Users receive AI-powered recommendations
milestones.description: AI recommendation engine with crowdsourced place verification, addressing the lack of solo-specific recommendations

milestones.title: Platform is ready for monetization and scale
milestones.description: Freemium pricing model, error tracking, and analytics infrastructure for sustainable growth

features.title: Offline-First Core Architecture
features.description: Implement comprehensive offline-first architecture with local data persistence, queued operations, and transparent sync status. The app remains fully functional without internet connectivity, automatically syncing when connection is restored.
features.rationale: Addresses critical user pain point: 'Lack of offline access to travel plans when internet is unavailable.' Also addresses competitor pain points: TripIt server downtime (pain-1-1), Wanderlog sync errors (pain-2-3), and general travel app offline functionality issues (gap-2). This is foundational for travelers in remote areas, flights, or regions with poor connectivity.
acceptance_criteria: App remains fully functional without internet connection
All data changes are queued and sync automatically when connection is restored
Clear visual indicators show offline/online status and sync progress
Local database persists all user data, trips, journals, and preferences
Conflict resolution handles concurrent edits gracefully
Users can read, create, edit, and delete all data types while offline
user_stories: As a solo traveler, I want to access my trip plans offline so that I can view my itinerary during flights or in remote areas without internet
As a traveler, I want the app to automatically save my changes locally so that I don't lose data if my connection drops
As a user, I want clear indicators of my sync status so that I know when my data is safe in the cloud

features.title: Robust Authentication & Token Refresh
features.description: Implement reliable authentication with AWS Cognito integration, robust token refresh mechanism with retry logic, session management, and graceful handling of expired tokens. Includes fallback mechanisms for offline authentication.
features.rationale: Addresses technical debt (token refresh marked as TODO) and critical competitor pain point: TripIt login problems where users get logged out and can't log back in (pain-1-2). Also addresses competitor authentication issues (pain-3-5). Reliable auth is foundational for all user-specific features.
acceptance_criteria: Users can log in, register, and reset passwords reliably
Token refresh happens automatically in the background without user awareness
Expired tokens trigger retry mechanism with exponential backoff
Users remain logged in across app restarts within session validity
Clear error messages guide users when authentication fails
Offline authentication allows access to cached data during network issues
user_stories: As a user, I want to stay logged in so that I don't have to repeatedly sign in
As a user, I want the app to handle token refresh automatically so that my session doesn't interrupt my usage
As a user, I want helpful error messages if login fails so that I know how to fix the issue

features.title: Trip Planning Workflow UI
features.description: Complete trip planning interface with day-by-day itinerary builder, drag-and-drop organization, time allocation, and activity management. Users can create multi-day trips, add destinations, accommodations, activities, and notes with an intuitive workflow.
features.rationale: Addresses core user goal: 'Plan comprehensive multi-day trips with itineraries, bookings, and logistics' and fills the gap left by Google Trips (pain-6-1, pain-6-2). This is the core value proposition - solo travelers need to plan trips without input from travel companions.
acceptance_criteria: Users can create new trips with name, dates, and destination
Users can add multiple days to trips with time-based itineraries
Drag-and-drop interface allows easy reordering of activities
Users can add accommodations, transportation, activities, and notes to each day
Trip details are automatically saved and synced across devices
Users can duplicate or delete trips and individual days
Time allocation shows clear daily schedules with duration for each activity
user_stories: As a solo traveler, I want to plan day-by-day itineraries so that I have a structured trip without needing input from others
As a planner, I want to drag and drop activities to rearrange my schedule so that I can easily optimize my time
As a traveler, I want to add all types of bookings and activities to one place so that I don't need multiple apps

features.title: Safety Check-in & Location Sharing
features.description: Safety-focused check-in system with automated check-ins, emergency contacts, real-time location sharing with trusted contacts, check-in scheduling, and missed check-in alerts. Includes emergency SOS functionality and status updates.
features.rationale: Addresses critical user pain point: 'Safety concerns when traveling solo without trusted contacts knowing your location.' Also addresses location sharing privacy concerns (pain-7-1, pain-7-2) by implementing privacy-first approach. This is a key differentiator from general travel apps.
acceptance_criteria: Users can designate trusted contacts from their phone or app community
Users can schedule automatic check-ins at specific times or locations
Trusted contacts receive location updates and status notifications
Missed check-ins trigger alerts to trusted contacts with last known location
Emergency SOS button shares real-time location with all trusted contacts
Users can manually update their status (safe, need help, emergency)
Location sharing is granular - users control what, when, and with whom
Battery-efficient location tracking prevents excessive drain
user_stories: As a solo traveler, I want to share my location with trusted contacts so that someone knows where I am in case of emergency
As a safety-conscious user, I want automated check-ins so that my contacts are alerted if I don't report in safely
As a traveler, I want an emergency SOS button so that I can quickly get help if needed
As a privacy-conscious user, I want to control exactly who sees my location and when

features.title: Sync & Conflict Resolution System
features.description: Robust data synchronization with conflict resolution for concurrent edits, operation queuing, retry mechanisms, and transparent sync status indicators. Handles edge cases like multiple device edits, stale data, and partial syncs.
features.rationale: Addresses critical competitor pain points: TripIt and Wanderlog sync errors (pain-1-1, pain-2-3) where users see 'There was a problem syncing changes' repeatedly. Reliable sync is essential for user trust, especially when offline-first architecture creates more opportunities for conflicts.
acceptance_criteria: All data changes sync automatically when internet is available
Concurrent edits from multiple devices are resolved with clear conflict UI
Failed sync attempts retry with exponential backoff
Users can manually trigger sync if needed
Sync status is visible in real-time (syncing, success, failed, pending)
Operation queue persists across app restarts
Users are notified of conflicts and can choose which version to keep
Sync failures provide actionable error messages
user_stories: As a user, I want my data to sync automatically so that changes on my phone appear on my tablet
As a user, I want clear feedback if sync fails so that I know whether my data is safe
As a user with multiple devices, I want conflict resolution options if I edit the same data on both devices

features.title: Performance Optimization for Large Trips
features.description: Optimize app performance for handling large, complex trips with hundreds of locations, activities, and photos. Implements lazy loading, data pagination, efficient rendering, virtual scrolling, and optimized data structures.
features.rationale: Addresses critical competitor pain points: Wanderlog severe performance issues with large trips (pain-2-1, pain-2-5) where 730 pins wouldn't load, and Roadtrippers lagging with more items (pain-2-5). Solo travelers planning long trips or multi-country adventures need performance that scales.
acceptance_criteria: App loads and renders smoothly with trips containing 500+ items
List views use virtual scrolling for memory efficiency
Images are lazy-loaded and compressed for optimal performance
Database queries are optimized with proper indexing
Map markers cluster intelligently for high-density areas
App startup time remains under 2 seconds even with large datasets
Memory usage stays within reasonable limits for mobile devices
user_stories: As a traveler planning a 3-month trip, I want the app to remain fast and responsive regardless of how many activities I add
As a user, I want maps to load quickly even with hundreds of saved locations
As a traveler, I want my photos to load efficiently without slowing down the app

features.title: Solo-Specific Destination Discovery
features.description: AI-powered destination and activity discovery specifically for solo travelers with safety ratings, solo-suitability filters, personalized recommendations, and comprehensive search. Includes popular solo destinations, hidden gems, and safety insights.
features.rationale: Addresses user pain point: 'Difficulty finding destinations, accommodations, and activities suitable for solo travelers.' Fills market gap (gap-7) - general travel apps don't filter for solo-suitable options. This is a key differentiator that leverages AI for solo-travel-specific insights.
acceptance_criteria: Search functionality filters destinations by solo-suitability ratings
Safety scores and insights visible for each destination
Personalized recommendations based on user preferences and past trips
Curated lists of popular solo destinations and hidden gems
Users can save destinations to their trips or wishlist
Filters for budget, activity level, safety, and solo-friendliness
Integration with trip planning for easy addition to itineraries
user_stories: As a solo traveler, I want to discover destinations that are safe and enjoyable for solo exploration
As a user, I want safety ratings and insights so that I can make informed decisions about where to travel
As a planner, I want personalized recommendations based on my travel style and preferences

features.title: Travel Journal with Media
features.description: Rich travel journal with photo and video uploads, text notes, location tagging, mood tracking, and media organization. Users can document daily experiences, add memories, and organize content by trip or date with automatic backups.
features.rationale: Addresses user pain point: 'Difficulty documenting and sharing travel experiences in one place.' Addresses competitor issues with clunky journal apps (pain-4-2) and crashes when adding photos (pain-2-2). Solo travelers value being able to document and share their adventures.
acceptance_criteria: Users can create journal entries with rich text formatting
Photos and videos upload in background with compression
Entries are automatically tagged with location and date
Users can organize entries by trip, date, or custom tags
Media gallery views all photos/videos from a trip
Offline editing with sync when connection available
Export or share entire trips or individual entries
Memory timeline shows chronological view of all travel experiences
user_stories: As a traveler, I want to document my daily experiences with photos and notes so that I can remember my trip
As a user, I want to organize my journal by trip so that I can easily find memories from specific adventures
As a traveler, I want to upload photos in the background so that I can continue using the app while they sync

features.title: Reliable Operation Queue with Persistence
features.description: Persistent operation queue system for offline operations that reliably queues, persists, and executes network operations when connection is restored. Handles retry logic, operation deduplication, and failure recovery.
features.rationale: Addresses technical debt (operation queue persistence marked as TODO) and competitor downtime issues (pain-1-1, pain-2-4). Essential for offline-first architecture - ensures no data loss and reliable operation execution even with poor connectivity.
acceptance_criteria: All write operations are queued when offline and executed when online
Queue persists across app restarts and device reboots
Operations are deduplicated to prevent redundant requests
Failed operations retry with exponential backoff up to a limit
Users can view pending operations and manually retry if needed
Queue priority ensures critical operations (like SOS) execute first
Clear UI shows pending, in-progress, and completed operations
user_stories: As a user, I want my changes to save even without internet so that I never lose data
As a traveler, I want the app to queue my updates and sync them automatically when I get wifi
As a user, I want transparency about what's waiting to sync so that I know my data is safe

features.title: Privacy-First Safety Features
features.description: Enhanced privacy controls for safety features including granular location sharing permissions, trusted contacts only, data minimization, clear privacy policies, and user-controlled data retention. Includes privacy dashboards and data export tools.
features.rationale: Addresses critical competitor pain points: location sharing privacy concerns (pain-7-1, pain-7-2, pain-7-3) and excessive permissions (pain-7-3). Privacy is essential for adoption, especially for solo travelers who are safety-conscious. This builds trust and differentiates from competitor apps.
acceptance_criteria: Users can choose exactly what data to share and with whom
Location sharing can be limited to specific times or durations
Trusted contacts must be explicitly approved by users
Privacy dashboard shows all shared data and allows easy revocation
Clear, simple privacy policy explains data usage
Minimal data collection - only what's necessary for features
Users can export or delete all their data on demand
Permission requests explain why access is needed and offer alternatives
user_stories: As a privacy-conscious user, I want to control exactly who sees my location and when
As a user, I want a privacy dashboard so that I can easily see and manage what I'm sharing
As a traveler, I want to understand what data is collected and why so that I can make informed choices

features.title: Google Maps Integration with Proper Navigation
features.description: Full Google Maps integration with reliable navigation, route optimization, place details, and real-time traffic. Maps maintain trip context during navigation and allow seamless switching between planning and navigation modes.
features.rationale: Addresses critical Roadtrippers pain point: map settings lost when starting new trip segments (pain-3-2) making it severely lacking as a navigation tool. Proper navigation integration is essential for travelers who need directions while maintaining trip context.
acceptance_criteria: Users can navigate to any location in their itinerary with one tap
Navigation maintains trip context - user can return to trip planning easily
Map settings and preferences persist across navigation sessions
Turn-by-turn navigation with voice guidance
Route optimization prevents backtracking between destinations
Real-time traffic and transit information
Offline map downloads for areas with poor connectivity
user_stories: As a traveler, I want to navigate to my planned activities without losing my trip context
As a user, I want map settings to persist so that I don't need to reconfigure them for each destination
As a traveler, I want optimized routes so that I don't waste time backtracking

features.title: Centralized Document Storage Management
features.description: Secure document storage and management system for travel documents including passports, visas, bookings, confirmations, insurance, and important contacts. Users can upload, categorize, and access all travel documents in one secure place.
features.rationale: Addresses user pain point: 'Managing multiple bookings, itineraries, and travel documents across different platforms.' Also addresses a known gap in current state - travel documentation storage not implemented. Convenience feature that saves users from juggling multiple apps and screenshots.
acceptance_criteria: Users can upload photos or PDFs of travel documents
Documents are categorized by type (passport, visa, booking, insurance, etc.)
Documents are linked to specific trips for easy access
Secure encryption protects sensitive document data
Offline access to important documents when traveling
Expiration alerts for passports, visas, and insurance
Quick access to emergency documents from home screen
user_stories: As a traveler, I want all my important documents in one secure place so that I don't have to search through emails and screenshots
As a user, I want my documents linked to trips so that I can easily find what I need while traveling
As a planner, I want expiration alerts so that I never let my passport or visa expire unexpectedly

features.title: Traveler Community & Connections
features.description: Community features for connecting with other solo travelers including verified profiles, location-based traveler discovery, interest-based matching, chat functionality, meetup organization, and safety features for in-person meetings.
features.rationale: Addresses user goal: 'Connect with other solo travelers for advice, companionship, or meetups.' Addresses competitor pain points: solo travel app safety concerns (pain-5-2) and high subscription costs (pain-5-1). Community is key differentiator but requires robust verification to build trust.
acceptance_criteria: Users can create verified profiles with travel interests and upcoming trips
Discover other travelers by location, destination, or travel dates
Interest-based matching suggests compatible travel companions
In-app chat for communication before meeting in person
Meetup creation for group activities or dining
Verification system builds trust (ID verification, social media links)
Safety features for meetups (public places, share location with contacts)
Clear platonic travel focus - not positioned as dating app
user_stories: As a solo traveler, I want to find others traveling to the same destination so that we can meet up or share tips
As a user, I want verified profiles and safety features so that I can feel comfortable connecting with strangers
As a traveler, I want to organize meetups so that I can enjoy experiences with others

features.title: AI-Powered Solo Recommendations
features.description: Advanced AI recommendation engine that learns from user preferences, past trips, journal entries, and community data to provide personalized destination, activity, and itinerary suggestions specifically for solo travelers with safety scoring.
features.rationale: Fills critical market gap (gap-7) - no solo-travel-specific recommendations. General travel apps don't filter for solo-suitable options. AI-powered recommendations based on solo travel patterns is a major differentiator that increases app value over time.
acceptance_criteria: AI learns from user behavior, preferences, and past trips
Personalized destination recommendations with solo-suitability scores
Activity suggestions based on interests and travel style
Itinerary templates that can be customized
Recommendations improve over time as more data is collected
Safety insights integrated into all recommendations
Community data enhances recommendations with proven destinations
Explainable AI - users can see why specific recommendations were made
user_stories: As a traveler, I want personalized recommendations so that I can discover destinations and activities I'll love
As a solo traveler, I want AI that understands solo travel safety and preferences
As a user, I want the app to learn my tastes so that recommendations get better over time

features.title: Freemium Pricing Model Implementation
features.description: Freemium business model with free tier offering essential features and premium tier with advanced features. Includes subscription management, feature gating, upgrade prompts, and flexible pricing (monthly/annual) to address competitor pricing pain points.
features.rationale: Addresses competitor pain points: expensive solo travel app subscriptions (pain-5-1) and unstable pricing (pain-4-1). Offering essential features free removes barrier for budget-conscious solo travelers while enabling sustainable revenue through premium features.
acceptance_criteria: Free tier includes trip planning, offline access, safety check-ins, and basic journal
Premium tier includes AI recommendations, community features, advanced analytics, and cloud storage
Flexible pricing with monthly and annual subscription options
Annual subscription offers discount equivalent to 2 months free
Clear feature comparison between free and premium tiers
Smooth upgrade prompts when free users access premium features
Secure payment processing with major payment methods
Subscription management with easy cancel and pause options
user_stories: As a budget-conscious traveler, I want essential features for free so that I can use the app without financial commitment
As a user, I want flexible monthly or annual options so that I can choose what works for my budget
As a premium user, I want advanced features that provide enough value to justify the subscription

features.title: Crowdsourced Place Verification System
features.description: Community-driven place verification where users can report if businesses are still open, permanently closed, or under new ownership. Includes voting system, regular database updates, and user feedback mechanisms to maintain data quality.
features.rationale: Addresses Roadtrippers pain point (pain-3-3): many listed places are out of business requiring users to verify independently. Also addresses market gap (gap-6) on data quality. Crowdsourcing keeps data fresh and builds community engagement.
acceptance_criteria: Users can report place status as open, closed, or moved
Verification requests require photo evidence or multiple confirmations
Voting system allows community to validate reports
Place status updates are reflected in search results
Users receive reputation points for accurate verifications
Regular automated database updates from trusted sources
Admin review for disputed verifications
Timestamps show when place information was last verified
user_stories: As a traveler, I want to know if a place is still open so that I don't waste time going there
As a community member, I want to report closed businesses so that other travelers don't have the same disappointment
As a user, I want to see when information was last verified so that I know how current it is

features.title: Error Tracking Integration
features.description: Comprehensive error tracking with Sentry or Firebase Crashlytics integration for production monitoring. Includes crash reporting, error aggregation, breadcrumbs, and alerting for proactive bug detection and resolution.
features.rationale: Addresses technical debt (error tracking integration incomplete - Sentry/Firebase Crashlytics commented out). Essential for production app to monitor stability, catch bugs early, and ensure quality experience for users. Part of technical improvement needed before scaling.
acceptance_criteria: All crashes and errors are automatically logged to tracking service
Breadcrumbs show user actions leading up to errors
Error aggregation groups similar issues for efficient triage
Alerts notify developers of critical errors or increasing error rates
Integration with existing logging infrastructure (AWS CloudWatch)
Privacy-sensitive data is excluded from error reports
Performance monitoring tracks app responsiveness and slow operations
Dashboard provides visibility into app health and user impact
user_stories: As a developer, I want automatic error tracking so that I can identify and fix issues before they affect many users
As a user, I want a stable app experience with minimal crashes and bugs
As a maintainer, I want visibility into app health so that I can prioritize improvements

features.title: Analytics & Event Tracking
features.description: Comprehensive analytics infrastructure tracking user behavior, feature usage, engagement metrics, and conversion funnels. Includes event tracking, user cohorts, retention analysis, and business intelligence for data-driven decisions.
features.rationale: Addresses technical debt (analytics incomplete - marked as TODO in route guard). Essential for understanding user behavior, measuring success metrics, and making data-driven product decisions. Needed for sustainable growth and optimization.
acceptance_criteria: Key user actions are tracked as events (trip created, check-in, journal entry, etc.)
User cohorts analyze behavior by signup date, demographics, or usage patterns
Retention metrics track DAU, MAU, and user生命周期
Conversion funnels measure onboarding and premium subscription flow
A/B testing framework for feature experiments
Privacy-compliant tracking with user consent and anonymization
Dashboard provides real-time insights into key metrics
Integration with success metrics from product vision
user_stories: As a product owner, I want analytics data so that I can understand how users engage with the app
As a developer, I want event tracking so that I can measure the impact of new features
As a business, I want conversion data so that I can optimize the freemium model
